offset-pagination.label: OFFSET and Pagination
offset-pagination.summary: Skip rows and paginate results with OFFSET.

offset-intro.title: What is OFFSET?
offset-intro.bodyMarkdown: When you want to skip a certain number of rows in your SQL query results, you can use the `OFFSET` clause. This is especially useful for pagination—showing results page by page. For example, if you want to display products 6–10, you can skip the first 5 rows using `OFFSET 5`.

offset-syntax.title: OFFSET Syntax
offset-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for using OFFSET with LIMIT is:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY id
LIMIT 5 OFFSET 5;
```

This query returns 5 rows, starting after skipping the first 5 rows (i.e., rows 6–10).

offset-pagination.title: Paginating Results
offset-pagination.bodyMarkdown:
Pagination is the process of dividing results into pages. For example, to show 2 products per page:
- Page 1: `LIMIT 2 OFFSET 0`
- Page 2: `LIMIT 2 OFFSET 2`
- Page 3: `LIMIT 2 OFFSET 4`

This helps users browse large datasets more easily.

offset-purpose-single-choice.title: Purpose of OFFSET
offset-purpose-single-choice.prompt: What is the main purpose of the OFFSET clause in SQL?

help.concept: OFFSET is used to skip a certain number of rows before starting to return rows from a query.
help.hint_1: OFFSET does not affect columns or sorting, but the starting point of rows.
help.hint_2: OFFSET is often used with LIMIT to paginate results.

options.a: To limit the number of columns returned
options.b: To skip a specified number of rows in the result
options.c: To sort the results in descending order

offset-pagination-multi-choice.title: Using OFFSET for Pagination
offset-pagination-multi-choice.prompt: Which of the following are true about using OFFSET for pagination? (Select all that apply)
offset-pagination-multi-choice.hint: Consider how OFFSET interacts with LIMIT and result order.

help.concept: OFFSET skips rows, and is commonly paired with LIMIT for pagination.
help.hint_1: OFFSET does not sort results, but skips rows after sorting.
help.hint_2: Pagination is achieved by combining LIMIT and OFFSET to select different sets of rows.

options.a: OFFSET is often used together with LIMIT
options.b: OFFSET can help display different pages of results
options.c: OFFSET changes the order of the results
options.d: OFFSET skips a set number of rows before returning results

offset-drag-reorder.title: Order the Pagination Steps
offset-drag-reorder.prompt: Arrange the steps to paginate SQL results using OFFSET and LIMIT.
offset-drag-reorder.hint: Think about the logical order of building a paginated query.

help.concept: Paginated queries start with SELECT, then sorting, then limiting and offsetting rows.
help.hint_1: You need to select data, sort it, then apply LIMIT and OFFSET.
help.hint_2: OFFSET is usually the last clause after LIMIT.

tokens.t1: Sort the results using ORDER BY
tokens.t2: Decide how many rows per page (LIMIT)
tokens.t3: Decide how many rows to skip (OFFSET)

offset-fill-blank-choice.title: Fill in the OFFSET Value
offset-fill-blank-choice.prompt: Suppose you want to display the third page of results, showing 2 products per page. What OFFSET value should you use?
offset-fill-blank-choice.hint: Each page skips (page number - 1) × page size rows.

help.concept: OFFSET skips a number of rows equal to (page number - 1) × page size.
help.hint_1: For page 3 with 2 rows per page, skip the missing term rows.
help.hint_2: OFFSET for page 3 is 2 × (3 - 1) = the missing term.

offset-fill-blank-choice.template: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY id LIMIT 2 OFFSET [___];

offset-sql-code-input.title: Write a Paginated Query
offset-sql-code-input.prompt: Write a SQL query to return the second page of products, showing 3 products per page, ordered by price ascending.
offset-sql-code-input.hint: Use LIMIT for page size and OFFSET to skip the first page.

help.concept: To paginate, use ORDER BY for sorting, LIMIT for page size, and OFFSET to skip rows from previous pages.
help.hint_1: For the second page, OFFSET should be equal to the page size.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY price ASC, LIMIT 3, OFFSET 3 will give you the second set of 3 products.
